# Copilot Instructions for oomlout_oobb_version_5

## Big picture (how files get made)
- `oobb_make_sets.py` builds a catalog of part definitions (`dict`s with `type`, size, extras).
- `oobb_base.get_thing_from_dict()` dispatches each definition to `oobb_get_items_oobb.py`, `oobb_get_items_other.py`, or `oobb_get_items_test.py` by naming convention: `get_<type>`.
- `oobb_base.get_default_thing()` computes IDs/metadata and writes `working.yaml` into the part folder.
- `oobb_base.build_thing()` / `build_things()` render `3dpr|laser|true` outputs (`.scad`, `.txt`, `.json`, `.yaml`) per part.
- Release scripts (`action_generate_release_3d_printable.py`, `action_generate_release_laser_cut.py`) copy built files into downstream release repo layouts.

## Key directories and boundaries
- `data/`: parameter tables used by get-item builders.
- `oobb_get_items_*.py`: geometry/component construction for each part family.
- `oobb_working_*.py`: ad-hoc “build this subset now” scripts; often call copy/export helpers.
- `working/`: CorelDRAW/backup artifacts; not a stable source of truth for tests.
- `templates/`: readme/release template assets.

## Developer workflows that matter here
- Full generation pipeline starts from `action_oobb_build_all.py`.
- Markdown docs are generated by `oobb_markdown.make_markdown()` from `parts/*/details.json`.
- Release packaging is file-copy based; no service/API layer exists.
- Some flows assume Windows absolute paths and optional external apps (`openscad`, CorelDRAW).

## Testing and snapshot workflow (baseline before refactors)
- Run: `python -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py"`
- Main suite: `tests/test_file_generation.py`
	- Covers file writers in `oobb_base` (`dump`, `load`, `build_thing`, `build_thing_filename`)
	- Covers release copy scripts and markdown generation paths
	- Uses temp directories + mocks to avoid heavy CAD renders
- Snapshot file: `tests/snapshots/file_generation_snapshot.json`
	- Compare mode is default
	- Update snapshots intentionally with env var `UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1`

## Project-specific conventions to follow
- Naming is behavior-driven: `action_*` (workflows), `oobb_working_*` (manual subsets), `oobb_get_items_*` (part constructors).
- Many scripts still reference `...version_4...` absolute paths; do not “clean up” paths blindly during refactors.
- Prefer adding kwargs-based path overrides (as done in release scripts) for testability instead of changing default behavior.
- Keep changes incremental: protect file outputs first, then refactor generation internals.

## Integration points
- Outputs are consumed by separate repos listed in `README.md` (3D print + laser releases).
- `oobb_dxf_laser_copy.py` and release scripts are the main cross-repo/file-distribution glue.
- External tool integration is shell-based (`openscad`, Corel workflows), so unit tests should mock those boundaries.
